recipes.name: Tacos al Pastor
recipes.description: Marinated pork tacos with pineapple, cilantro, and onion.
steps: Soak guajillo chiles in hot water for 15 minutes, then blend with garlic, pineapple juice, vinegar, cumin, oregano, and salt.
Marinate the pork in this mixture for at least 4 hours, ideally overnight.
Cook the marinated pork on a hot griddle or skillet until browned and slightly crispy.
Warm the tortillas on a dry skillet.
Fill tortillas with pork, top with diced pineapple, onion, and cilantro.
Serve with lime wedges.

recipes.name: Pozole Rojo
recipes.description: Traditional hominy soup with pork in a rich red chile broth.
ingredients: Shredded cabbage, radishes, lime, and onion for garnish
steps: Boil pork in a large pot with half the onion and 2 garlic cloves until tender, about 1.5 hours.
Toast and soak the dried chiles, then blend with remaining onion, garlic, and oregano.
Strain the chile mixture into the pork broth.
Add hominy and simmer for another 30 minutes.
Season with salt.
Serve hot, topped with shredded cabbage, radishes, lime, and chopped onion.

recipes.name: Chiles Rellenos
recipes.description: Roasted poblano peppers stuffed with cheese, battered and fried.
ingredients: 8 oz queso Oaxaca or Monterey Jack, sliced
steps: Roast poblanos over an open flame until charred, then peel off the skin.
Make a slit in each pepper and remove seeds, stuff with cheese.
Beat egg whites until stiff, then fold in yolks to make a batter.
Dust stuffed peppers with flour, dip in egg batter, and fry until golden.
Make a simple tomato sauce by sautéing onion and garlic, then simmering with tomato sauce.
Serve fried peppers topped with warm tomato sauce.

recipes.name: Feijoada
recipes.description: Hearty black bean stew with assorted meats, a Brazilian classic.
ingredients: 2 cups dried black beans, soaked overnight
Cooked white rice and orange slices for serving
steps: Drain soaked beans and place in a large pot with bay leaves and enough water to cover.
Simmer beans for about 1 hour until starting to soften.
In a separate pan, brown the bacon, then add onion and garlic and sauté until fragrant.
Add the sausage and pork ribs to the beans, along with the bacon mixture.
Simmer everything together for about 1.5 hours until meats are tender and beans are creamy.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve over white rice with orange slices on the side.

recipes.name: Brigadeiro
recipes.description: Sweet chocolate truffle balls, a favorite Brazilian dessert.
steps: Combine condensed milk, cocoa powder, and butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
Stir constantly for about 10-15 minutes until the mixture thickens and pulls away from the pan.
Pour onto a greased plate and let cool completely.
Grease your hands with butter and roll the mixture into small balls.
Roll each ball in chocolate sprinkles.
Serve in small paper cups.

recipes.name: Pão de Queijo
recipes.description: Chewy Brazilian cheese bread made with tapioca flour.
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Heat milk, oil, and salt in a pot until just boiling.
Pour the hot mixture over the tapioca flour and stir to combine.
Let cool slightly, then mix in eggs one at a time, followed by the cheese.
The batter will be sticky; spoon into greased muffin tins.
Bake for 18-20 minutes until puffed and golden on the outside.

recipes.name: Arepas
recipes.description: Grilled corn cakes, often filled with cheese or meat.
steps: In a bowl, mix the corn meal and salt.
Gradually add warm water and oil, mixing until a smooth dough forms.
Let the dough rest for 5 minutes.
Divide into 4-6 balls and flatten into discs about 1/2 inch thick.
If desired, add cheese in the center and fold the dough around it.
Cook on a hot griddle for 5-7 minutes per side until golden with light char marks.

recipes.name: Ajiaco Bogotano
recipes.description: Creamy chicken and potato soup with corn and herbs from Bogotá.
ingredients: 3 ears of corn, cut into pieces
2 lbs mixed potatoes (papa criolla, russet, and red)
1 bunch guascas herb (or substitute with cilantro)
Heavy cream, capers, and avocado for serving
steps: In a large pot, bring chicken broth to a boil and add chicken breasts, onion, and garlic.
Simmer until chicken is cooked through, then remove and shred the chicken.
Add the corn pieces and potatoes to the broth, simmering until potatoes begin to break down and thicken the soup.
Return shredded chicken to the pot and add the guascas herb.
Simmer for another 15-20 minutes until creamy in texture.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot with a drizzle of heavy cream, capers, and avocado slices on the side.

recipes.name: Bandeja Paisa
recipes.description: A hearty platter with beans, rice, meat, plantain, and avocado.
steps: Cook the red beans with seasonings until tender (or use pre-cooked beans).
Prepare white rice separately.
Grill the steak with salt and pepper to your preferred doneness.
Fry the chorizo and bacon until cooked through.
Fry the plantain slices until golden and caramelized.
Fry the eggs sunny-side up.
Arrange beans, rice, steak, chorizo, bacon, plantain, egg, and avocado on a large plate.
Serve with arepas on the side.

recipes.name: Empanadas de Carne
recipes.description: Baked pastries filled with seasoned beef, onions, and spices.
steps: Sauté onion and bell pepper until soft.
Add ground beef and cook until browned, season with cumin, paprika, chili flakes, salt, and pepper.
Let the filling cool, then mix in chopped hard-boiled eggs.
Place a spoonful of filling onto each dough disc, fold over, and seal edges with the beaten egg.
Crimp edges with a fork.
Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 20-25 minutes until golden brown.

recipes.name: Asado Argentino
recipes.description: Classic Argentine grilled beef short ribs and sausages.
ingredients: Chimichurri sauce (parsley, garlic, oregano, vinegar, oil, chili flakes)
steps: Prepare a charcoal or wood grill, allowing coals to reach medium heat.
Season ribs generously with coarse salt.
Place ribs bone-side down on the grill, cooking slowly over indirect heat for about 1-1.5 hours.
Add sausages to the grill in the last 20 minutes of cooking.
Prepare chimichurri by combining chopped parsley, garlic, oregano, vinegar, oil, and chili flakes.
Slice the ribs and serve with sausages and chimichurri sauce.

recipes.name: Milanesa Napolitana
recipes.description: Breaded beef cutlets topped with tomato sauce, ham, and cheese.
steps: Season beef cutlets with salt and pepper.
Dredge each cutlet in flour, dip in beaten egg, then coat with breadcrumbs.
Fry cutlets in hot oil until golden and cooked through.
Place fried cutlets on a baking tray, top each with tomato sauce, a slice of ham, and mozzarella.
Bake at 400°F (200°C) for about 10 minutes until cheese melts.
Serve hot with fries or salad.

recipes.name: Ceviche Peruano
recipes.description: Fresh white fish marinated in lime juice with onion and chili.
ingredients: 1 lb fresh white fish (sea bass or tilapia), cut into cubes
Sweet potato and corn for serving
steps: Place fish cubes in a bowl and season with salt and pepper.
Pour lime juice over the fish, ensuring it's fully covered.
Add sliced onion, chili, and minced garlic.
Let marinate for 10-15 minutes until the fish turns opaque (cooked by the acid).
Sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Serve immediately with boiled sweet potato and corn on the side.

recipes.name: Lomo Saltado
recipes.description: Stir-fried beef with onions, tomatoes, and fries, served with rice.
ingredients: 1 lb beef sirloin, cut into strips
steps: Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat with oil.
Sear the beef strips quickly until browned but still tender, then set aside.
In the same pan, sauté garlic and onion for 1-2 minutes.
Add tomatoes and stir-fry briefly.
Return beef to the pan, add soy sauce and vinegar, and toss everything together quickly.
Fold in cooked French fries at the very end.
Serve immediately with white rice, garnished with cilantro.

recipes.name: Ají de Gallina
recipes.description: Creamy shredded chicken in a spicy yellow chili sauce.
ingredients: 1 lb chicken breast, cooked and shredded
3 aji amarillo peppers (or yellow chili paste)
Cooked white rice and boiled potatoes for serving
Hard-boiled eggs and black olives for garnish
steps: Soak bread slices in milk until soft, then blend until smooth.
Sauté onion and garlic until translucent, add aji amarillo paste and cook for a few minutes.
Stir in the bread-milk mixture and simmer until thickened.
Add shredded chicken, Parmesan cheese, and chopped nuts.
Simmer for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until creamy.
Serve over rice with boiled potatoes, garnished with hard-boiled egg and olives.

recipes.name: Pabellón Criollo
recipes.description: Shredded beef, black beans, rice, and fried plantain - the national dish.
steps: Boil the flank steak with a bit of onion and garlic until very tender, about 1 hour.
Shred the cooked beef with two forks.
Sauté onion, bell pepper, and garlic, then add the shredded beef along with cumin, salt, and pepper.
Cook the black beans with a bit of the cooking broth, onion, and garlic until flavorful.
Fry the plantain slices until golden on both sides.
Serve the shredded beef, black beans, white rice, and fried plantain together on one plate.

recipes.name: Tequeños
recipes.description: Crispy fried cheese sticks wrapped in dough - a beloved party snack.
ingredients: 1 lb queso blanco or mozzarella, cut into sticks
steps: Mix flour, salt, and sugar in a bowl, then work in the softened butter.
Gradually add water and knead until a smooth, elastic dough forms.
Let the dough rest for 15 minutes, then roll out thin and cut into long strips.
Wrap each cheese stick with a strip of dough, slightly overlapping, sealing the edges.
Heat oil to medium-high heat and fry the wrapped cheese sticks until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes.
Drain on paper towels and serve warm.

recipes.name: Cachapas
recipes.description: Sweet corn pancakes filled with soft cheese.
ingredients: 4 cups fresh or frozen sweet corn kernels
Queso de mano or mozzarella cheese for filling
steps: Blend corn kernels with cornmeal, sugar, salt, and milk until you get a thick, slightly chunky batter.
Heat a griddle or skillet and add a small amount of butter.
Pour batter to form pancakes about 5-6 inches wide.
Cook for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Place a slice of cheese on one half of the pancake while warm, fold over, and let the cheese melt slightly.
Serve warm.

recipes.name: Empanadas de Pino
recipes.description: Baked beef empanadas with onions, olives, and hard-boiled egg.
steps: Sauté onions slowly until soft and slightly caramelized.
Add ground beef and cook until browned, season with cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Let the filling cool completely (this is important for Chilean-style empanadas).
Place a portion of filling on each dough disc, add a slice of egg, an olive, and a few raisins.
Fold the dough into a half-moon shape and seal edges, crimping with a fork.
Brush with beaten egg and bake at 400°F (200°C) for 25-30 minutes until golden.

recipes.name: Pastel de Choclo
recipes.description: Corn casserole layered with seasoned beef and chicken.
ingredients: 6 cups fresh or frozen corn kernels
steps: Blend the corn kernels with milk until you get a thick paste, then cook with butter in a pot until thickened, stirring constantly.
Sauté onion until soft, add ground beef and season with paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper, cook until browned.
In a baking dish, layer the beef mixture, shredded chicken, sliced eggs, and olives.
Pour the corn mixture over the top, spreading evenly.
Sprinkle a thin layer of sugar on top for a slightly caramelized crust.
Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 30-35 minutes until the top is golden.
